Hey everyone, honestly didn't know this channel existed until now,  I'm a CS fresher... a real beginner just starting out into this field of DevOps and I plan to get rlly serious abt it, I feel my learning journey would go way faster and more efficient if I had study buddies or like minded beginners who'll keep each other accountable, share resources, discuss abt projects done , and participate in online competitions...anyone interested can dm me !

Bro if you are in search of these kind of people to go on with your journey then ig u are probably waiting for too long, i would suggest u  begin with your own and share resources your thoughts, learnings people will gradually connect and join
Yup...I have ofcourse started but I just wanted to find ppl who'd study with me and keep pushing me to grind more, we'll see if there r many more like me...thanks bro

Hey anyone here tried chaos engineering or stress testing on their AWS infrastructure ?

hey everyone, i am currently getting into the MERN stack after learning the html,css,js. Once I build a few full-stack projects, my long-term goal is to get into DevOps.

I want to start practicing a bit of DSA on the side so my skills compound over time. I asked gemini what language to pick, and it suggested Python since it overlaps heavily with DevOps scripting.

When I asked why I shouldn't just stick with JS, it pointed out that JS lacks built-in data structures (like heaps) and has weird default sorting that can mess you up in timed interviews. 
It also mentioned that Go (which roadmap.sh recommends) is better saved for actual cloud infrastructure later, rather than whiteboard interviews right now.

Just wanted a sanity check from real developers: is Python definitely the smartest choice for my DSA practice given my MERN-to-DevOps roadmap?
Anyone there?
When you guys are active?

yes that is true kinda
for now pick a path and work on it (what you have chosen works fine for now)
use python or whatever you want to use doesnt really matter in dsa. js thing is true btw
